node_types.description: An individual involved in therapy, including patients, therapists, and authors.

properties.name: role
properties.description: Role in therapy or authorship (e.g., patient, therapist, author)

properties.name: cultural_background
properties.description: Cultural or religious background, if relevant

supported_by_chapters: 2. The Role of Emotion and the Nature of the Therapeutic Relationship
12. Modifications for Different Settings and Populations

node_types.description: A clinical diagnosis or mental health condition assigned to a patient.

properties.name: diagnosis_name
properties.description: Name of the diagnosis (e.g., Major Depressive Disorder)

properties.name: diagnostic_criteria
properties.description: Criteria or description of the diagnosis

node_types.description: A psychological, emotional, behavioral, or physiological symptom experienced by a patient.
supported_by_chapters: 4. The Initial Session: Providing a Cognitive Rationale

node_types.description: A cognitive construct relevant to therapy, such as automatic thoughts, core beliefs, schemas, or cognitive distortions.

properties.name: concept_type
properties.description: Type of cognitive concept (e.g., automatic thought, core belief, schema, cognitive distortion)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description or definition of the concept

supported_by_chapters: 7. Schemas: Core Beliefs and Underlying Assumptions
13. Common Problems Encountered in Cognitive Therapy

node_types.description: A specific intervention, method, or tool used in therapy.

properties.name: technique_name
properties.description: Name of the technique (e.g., Socratic questioning, Thought Record)

supported_by_chapters: 7. Schemas: Core Beliefs and Underlying Assumptions

node_types.description: A single session or encounter in the course of therapy.

properties.name: agenda
properties.description: Planned topics or goals for the session

supported_by_chapters: 4. The Initial Session: Providing a Cognitive Rationale

node_types.description: A structured plan outlining therapeutic goals, interventions, and strategies for a patient.

properties.name: cultural_considerations
properties.description: Cultural or religious factors considered in planning

supported_by_chapters: 4. The Initial Session: Providing a Cognitive Rationale
12. Modifications for Different Settings and Populations

node_types.description: A standardized assessment tool or scale used to evaluate symptoms, risk, or progress.

properties.name: measure_name
properties.description: Name of the measure (e.g., BDI, PHQ-9, Hopelessness Scale)

properties.name: administration_frequency
properties.description: How often the measure is administered

supported_by_chapters: 4. The Initial Session: Providing a Cognitive Rationale

node_types.description: A pharmacological treatment used in the management of depression or comorbid conditions.
A context or environment in which therapy is delivered (e.g., outpatient, inpatient, group, primary care).

properties.name: setting_type
properties.description: Type of setting (e.g., outpatient, inpatient, group, primary care)

properties.name: modifications
properties.description: Modifications to therapy required for this setting

supported_by_chapters: 12. Modifications for Different Settings and Populations

node_types.description: A specific patient group or demographic relevant to therapy (e.g., children, adolescents, older adults, cultural groups).

properties.name: population_type
properties.description: Type of population (e.g., children, adolescents, older adults, specific cultural group)

properties.name: special_considerations
properties.description: Special considerations for therapy with this population

supported_by_chapters: 12. Modifications for Different Settings and Populations

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person (patient) has been assigned a specific diagnosis.
Links a diagnosis to its associated symptoms.
supported_by_chapters: 4. The Initial Session: Providing a Cognitive Rationale

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person is currently experiencing a symptom.

properties.name: severity_rating
properties.description: Severity of the symptom for this person

supported_by_chapters: 4. The Initial Session: Providing a Cognitive Rationale

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person holds or exhibits a particular cognitive concept (e.g., core belief, automatic thought).
supported_by_chapters: 7. Schemas: Core Beliefs and Underlying Assumptions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person (patient or therapist) participated in a specific therapeutic session.

properties.name: role
properties.description: Role in the session (e.g., patient, therapist, family member)

edge_types.description: Indicates that a specific therapeutic technique was used during a session.

properties.name: purpose
properties.description: Purpose of using the technique in this session

edge_types.description: Indicates that a technique was assigned as homework in a session.

properties.name: homework_instructions
properties.description: Instructions or details of the homework assignment

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person is following a specific treatment plan.

properties.name: adherence_level
properties.description: Level of adherence to the plan

supported_by_chapters: 4. The Initial Session: Providing a Cognitive Rationale

edge_types.description: Indicates that a treatment plan is designed to address a specific symptom.
supported_by_chapters: 4. The Initial Session: Providing a Cognitive Rationale

edge_types.description: Indicates that a treatment plan includes a specific therapeutic technique.
supported_by_chapters: 7. Schemas: Core Beliefs and Underlying Assumptions

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person has been assessed using a specific measure or scale.
supported_by_chapters: 4. The Initial Session: Providing a Cognitive Rationale

edge_types.description: Indicates that a diagnosis is treated with a specific medication.

properties.name: treatment_phase
properties.description: Phase of treatment (e.g., acute, maintenance, relapse prevention)

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person has been prescribed a specific medication.
Indicates the setting in which a therapeutic session took place.
supported_by_chapters: 12. Modifications for Different Settings and Populations

edge_types.description: Indicates that a person is part of a specific population or demographic group.
supported_by_chapters: 12. Modifications for Different Settings and Populations

edge_types.description: Indicates that a treatment plan takes into account cultural or population-specific considerations.

properties.name: cultural_alignment_notes
properties.description: Notes on how the plan is adapted for cultural or religious alignment

supported_by_chapters: 12. Modifications for Different Settings and Populations
